content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to accept 5 elements in an array.
Then traverse the array and display all elements using a loop.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of elements in the array.
The second lind of input contains NNN integers separated by a space denoting the elements of the array.


Output Format
Print the elements of the array separated by a space.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 5), the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N integers separated by spaces (0 ≤ each element ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the N integers in the same order, separated by a single space. Print a newline after each test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case with N=5; the program prints the five numbers unchanged.
One test case with N=3; the three numbers are echoed.
Single‑element array; the sole value is output.

solution.approach: The task is only to echo the input array. For each test case we read N, then read the N integers into a list and print them in the order they were read. No computation is required beyond I/O. Using BufferedReader with StringTokenizer gives O(total N) time and O(N) auxiliary space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(ΣN) where ΣN is the total number of integers across all test cases (at most 500).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for storing the current array.
